heading: Exemption from arrest or seizure
label: 30908 Exemption from arrest or seizure
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)3090846 App.:741 (less 11th–26th words).(less 11th–26th words), ; , ; , , .Mar. 9, 1920, ch. 95, § 141 Stat. 525Sept. 26, 1950, ch. 1049, § 2(a)(2)64 Stat. 1038Pub. L. 96–70, § 3(b)(5)93 Stat. 455Sept. 27, 1979The words “a federally-owned corporation” are substituted for “any corporation in which the United States or its representatives shall own the entire outstanding capital stock” because of the definition of “federally-owned corporation” in section 30902 of the revised title. The words “after ” are omitted as obsolete. The words “in view of the provision herein made for a libel in personam” are omitted as unnecessary. The words “or its possessions” are omitted because of the definition of “United States” in chapter 1 of the revised title. The words “, That this chapter shall not apply to the Panama Canal Commission” are omitted because the Commission has been dissolved. See .March 9, 1920Provided22 U.S.C. 3714a

children.text: A vessel owned by, possessed by, or operated by or for the United States or a federally-owned corporation.
Cargo owned or possessed by the United States or a federally-owned corporation.
